Intraday post (08/Jan)

Weekly post:

Challenges for 2026 | Weekly post (05 - 09/Jan)

I dropped a big post about the yearly outlook of challenges and market/economy risks:

The challenges of 2026

In the post that follows, I have compiled the geopolitical, economic, and equity-market developments and challenges that have embedded significant—yet still underpriced—risk in the market.

Spend some time with it! Very important piece.

Weekly upside target was hit with reversion back to the expected price levels into Thursday, where I said “profit taking will occur”.

Tomorrow it is NFP day. The sentiment is “bad news are good news”.

I also dropped a new educational post yesterday where I put speed profile into context:

Liquidity structure | Let's put speed profile into context

Let’s look at the daily positioning…

INTRADAY POST Coding today’s positioning:

The net speed profile shows a fly pattern: customers are buying volatility into both direction below and above the centroid of 6915.49But like 2x more volatility is being bought upside than to the downside, creating a net flat-to-negative speed profile. Vvol is sold into both direction.

Upside pivot is at 6922.84with target of 6947.95 (6970- 6942 range)The pivot is very sticky, and volatility really needs to catch up to break above it. Into the PM session, the upside target becomes more rejecting. However if 6940/45 can be hold into 1.30PM, 6970 becomes magnetic.

Downside pivot is at 6904.36with target of 6854.13This would provide us a reversion opportunity back towards the centroid and stabilize there after 2PM. If the reversion is too slow (i mean, i doesn’t happen within 1-1.5 hours) 6885 will be a pin.

The distribution is almost balanced, slightly more chance to the upside (+7.24%.. not much tho).So market sees slightly less resistance to move up, but the main bet remains rangebound around the centroid.

OptionsDepth Heatmap
